ocr.text: The data presented in Table 1 offers a comprehensive overview of the impact of varying time intervals on the measured effects
within the specified scenarios.
Group  Dose (mg/kg)  Reaction time in (sec)
0 min  15 min  30 min  60 min  90 min
Specifically, the metrics for "Nb of QTL" and "Type of effect" are critical in understanding the genetic architecture being
studied.
Scenario  Nb of QTL  Type of effect  MAF  Linkage disequilibrium*
1  1  Additive  0.3  0.8
2  1  Additive  0.3  0.4
3  1  Additive  0.1  0.8
4  1  Dominant  0.3  0.8
5  2  Additive  0.3  0.8
The results indicate that as time progresses from 0 to 90 minutes, there is a noticeable decrease in the observed effects, as
reflected in the values of 3.02 ± 0.12 at 0 minutes, tapering to 2.85 ± 0.10 by 90 minutes.
Vendor  CASS  Street Offset  Corner Inset  Street Data Files  Scheduled Data File Updates  Original Datuma  Manual Address Cleaningb
TIGER  USPS  Other
A  Yes  40 ft  Yes  2002  2004  Yes  4×/yr  WGS84  No
B  No  5 ft  Yes  2002  2004  Yes  4×/yr  NAD83  No
C  Yes  50 ft  No  2002  2004  Yes  6×/yr  NAD83  Yes
D  No  0 ft  No  2002  2003  No  2×/yr  NAD83  No
This trend suggests a potential time-dependent decay in the additive effects of quantitative trait loci (QTL), which could have
implications for the interpretation of genetic influence over time. Furthermore, the data highlights the varying levels of minor
allele frequency (MAF) across different scenarios, underscoring the importance of genetic variation in the observed effects. The
insights gleaned from this table may inform future research directions and enhance our understanding of the dynamic interplay
between genetic factors and temporal variables in various biological contexts.
